rationale: Spec Lock must pin schema versions and SSOT hashes to prevent agent drift.
Self-build plan nodes and user-project sharded plan nodes have different validation and acceptance needs; schemas are kept separate and disambiguated in Glossary/Crosswalk.
GitHub CLI is forbidden; GitHub operations are performed via HTTPS API with OAuth device-code as the default interactive flow.
Acceptance requires a linter to validate evidence.json against evidence.schema.json; verifier adds validate-evidence subcommand.
Plans/GitHub_API_Auth_and_Flows.md changed during Pass 2 doc hardening; update Spec Lock sha256 to preserve GATE-002 integrity deterministically.
Spec Lock gate integrity requires canonical_ssot_hashes to match current SSOT file contents after registering new UI command IDs and reconciling existing Crosswalk drift.

Spec Lock protocol requires refreshed SSOT hashes after canonical-doc and verifier updates; run-gates coverage is aligned to script-enforceable gates with non-example node evidence/change-budget checks.

Executor ordering/completion semantics were made deterministic across self-build and sharded plan graphs; Spec Lock hashes were refreshed and Executor_Protocol was added to canonical lock coverage so GATE-002 remains deterministic.

requirement_refs is optional (not required) so that existing plan nodes remain valid without modification; traceability is enforced at the coverage report level via requirements_coverage.schema.json rather than at individual node validation time, enabling incremental adoption without breaking pre-existing nodes.

GATE-011 enforces full requirement-to-node-and-check coverage so every requirement ID is traceable to at least one plan node and one verification check; GATE-012 enforces quality verdict==PASS on the requirements_quality_report artifact and mandates UI escalation (BLOCKED-state promotion) whenever needs_user_clarification is non-empty, preventing silent acceptance of ambiguous requirements; GATE-013 enforces that no unresolved <!-- AMBIGUOUS: <id> <description> --> markers remain in any tracked document without a paired auto_decisions.jsonl entry that records the resolution, closing the loop between ambiguity discovery and authoritative decision; pm.requirements_quality_report.schema.v1 is the machine-verifiable JSON contract for quality gate artifacts produced by the requirements analysis phase, enabling deterministic gate evaluation without human re-reading; the HTML-comment marker format <!-- AMBIGUOUS: <id> <description> --> was chosen for cross-format compatibility because it is invisible in rendered Markdown, safely embeddable in JSON strings, and parseable by a single regex across all document types.

Requirements traceability and quality artifacts/gates must be internally consistent and schema-aligned; quality report and coverage outputs are pinned as verification-canonical derived artifacts, quickstart remains optional derived-only, and Spec Lock hashes are refreshed so verifier results reflect current SSOT state deterministically.

Role terminology in Plans was normalized from Executor to Overseer where it denotes the controlling orchestration role; compatibility references to the canonical file path Executor_Protocol.md were preserved. Canonical SSOT hashes were refreshed in Spec Lock to keep gate evaluation deterministic.
Plans/UI_Command_Catalog.md content changed during rebrand cleanup; refresh Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes deterministically so GATE-002 integrity checks pass.

UI scaling migration from Iced token-multiplication to Slint native scale factor was documented in FinalGUISpec and rewrite-tie-in-memo but lacked a formal contract in Contracts_V0.md and missing ContractRef tags on normative statements. Added §8 UI Scaling contract, ContractRef tags to FinalGUISpec §16.2 and rewrite-tie-in-memo, and Crosswalk §3.15 UIScaling entry.

The Context Injection feature must be deterministic and low-bloat by default. Default all three injectors ON (Parent Summary, scoped AGENTS.md chain, Attempt Journal) to preserve intent and prevent repeated failures; enforce small, fixed budgets (parent_summary hard-capped to 10 lines; AGENTS.md authoring-time budgets 8KB/80 lines/6 headings) so instruction files remain light and promotion cannot accrete wiki content; attempt journal injection remains single-most-recent only, hard-capped to 4KB or 60 lines (truncate deterministically if exceeded); strict mode defaults to WARN-only to avoid blocking runs unexpectedly.

Locked SSOT docs Plans/Contracts_V0.md and Plans/Project_Output_Artifacts.md were modified to add Context Injection / workspace sidecar semantics and contracts. Refresh Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes deterministically so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains consistent.

Rebrand and recovery planning artifacts were completed and were generating folder bloat and stale cross-links; removing them reduces drift surface. Because Crosswalk is a Spec_Lock pinned SSOT file, its sha256 was refreshed in Spec_Lock so GATE-002 remains deterministic after removing the RECOVERY_* reference.

Assistant memory integration requires deterministic defaults for physical store separation, capsule budget, retrieval cap, half-life decay, and strict Assistant-only access boundaries so memory remains low-bloat, project-scoped, and non-conflated with rules. Defaults were fixed in one SSOT and referenced by dependent plans to avoid duplication and ambiguous runtime behavior.

Set deterministic verification/injection defaults for Evidence-Backed Gists: Verified-only capsule + retrieval injection is ON by default; unverified auto-save remains enabled (assistant.memory.auto_save_unverified=true) but does not relax injection gating; GUI manual Verify must be evidence-backed (no arbitrary Verified marking without recomputable EvidenceRefs); AutoRunBoundary and AutoMilestone triggers are each rate-limited to 1 invocation per run and deduplicated (embed_text_hash / (gist_id,evidence_ref)) to prevent duplicate writes. Capsule budget (350 tokens) and max injected items (5) remain as previously set by dec-2026-02-26-assistant-memory-subsystem-defaults.

Assistant-memory command wiring must be deterministic across chat and memory SSOT docs. Command IDs are locked to `cmd.chat.memory.*` and the broken storage validation citation is replaced with verifier/evidence-based validation text so references remain resolvable without creating new plan files.

`Plans/UI_Command_Catalog.md` is hash-pinned by Spec Lock. Registering canonical `cmd.chat.memory.*` IDs changes the file content, so canonical_ssot_hashes must be refreshed to keep GATE-002 deterministic and passing.

Document packaging needed deterministic, non-bypassable defaults for triggers, split behavior, naming, and three full audits. Locked defaults: max_bytes=262144, max_estimated_tokens=65536 with token estimate char_count/4, fallback_chunk_lines=400, primary split at Markdown h2 outside fences, fallback deterministic fixed line chunks, and audit_count=3. Spec Lock canonical hashes were refreshed for all changed SSOT files so GATE-002 stays deterministic and reproducible.

Document Set on-disk path convention must be deterministic: when packaging triggers fire, the canonical form is a `.docset/` directory adjacent to the original file path, the original file becomes a derived pointer stub with fixed fields (entrypoint, source_sha256, verify_command), and verification must enforce pointer-stub/docset consistency. This prevents divergent implementations from inventing incompatible path layouts or omitting the pointer stub.
Plans/00-plans-index.md changed due to expanded plan shard indexes; refresh Spec Lock sha256 so GATE-002 remains deterministic and passing.

Codex, GitHub Copilot, and Gemini are DirectApi providers and do not use CLI or local experimental toggles; Cursor and Claude Code are CliBridge providers with no experimental spec; OpenCode is a ServerBridge provider and is out of scope for per-platform CLI experimental toggles. The Slint rewrite must remove GUI-level experimental feature sections, per-platform experimentalEnabled config keys, and experimental CLI flags so providers expose only stable capabilities via their APIs.
Plan SSOT docs changed (index edits + shard-index regeneration). Refresh Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes deterministically so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains reproducible and passing.

Plans/Media_Generation_and_Capabilities.md was updated to fix deterministic envelope mapping, capability invocation wording, extraction guard semantics, Appendix alignment, appendix-level ContractRef compliance, and cross-section size_k normalization consistency. Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes must be refreshed so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.

Corrective Prompt 1 pass: (1) AC-MED04 now respects admin toggles and model availability instead of unconditionally enabling all four media kinds; (2) media.generate response upgraded from outputs[]/artifact_path to artifacts[]/artifact:// with request_id, engine.backend, manifest.json, sha256, usage.estimated_cost_usd; (3) error codes normalized to 9 stable codes (NOT_CONFIGURED replaces MISSING_GOOGLE_KEY, PROVIDER_ERROR replaces GENERATION_FAILED, INTERNAL_ERROR added); (4) model_override regex whitespace before lookahead removed in both main and appendix; (5) stale 'all five platforms' wording neutralized in touched docs. Spec Lock hashes refreshed for modified SSOT files.

Plans/orchestrator-subagent-integration.md was updated to remove stale 'All 5 platforms' wording and align cross-provider language with the 6-provider split. Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes must be refreshed so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.

Plans/interview-subagent-integration.md contained Gemini command-line mechanics (versions and experimental agent toggles). Gemini is direct-provider only in the rewrite. This change removes those mechanics, keeps Gemini as a model provider, and refreshes Spec Lock hashes so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.

AC-MED15 incorrectly said 'eight' stable error codes while §2.6 defines nine. This change corrects the count to 'nine' and refreshes Spec Lock hashes so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.

Plans/orchestrator-subagent-integration.md still contained Gemini command-line plan-mode mechanics (legacy plan-mode flags and settings). Gemini is direct-provider only in the rewrite. This change removes those mechanics, replaces them with Direct-provider checks (API key + models.list + capability gating), and refreshes Spec Lock hashes so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.

Media SSOT model_override regex needed to allow multi-word aliases while also removing a literal space character from the allowed model token class; replaced the literal space in the character class with \s and refreshed Spec Lock sha256 so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.

Plans/Media_Generation_and_Capabilities.md contains a normative requirement in §2.6; the ContractRef must appear within the verifier's bounded lookahead window. Inserted a ContractRef immediately after the MUST sentence and refreshed Spec Lock sha256 so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.

Plans/Glossary.md was updated to include OpenCode in provider enumeration and align canonical provider split wording. Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes must be refreshed so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.
Plans/orchestrator-subagent-integration.md current content hash differed from Spec Lock; refreshed canonical_ssot_hashes entry to restore deterministic GATE-002 integrity.

Updated provider split wording and Gemini media model usage in allowlisted Plans docs, including pinned SSOT files (orchestrator-subagent-integration.md and Media_Generation_and_Capabilities.md). Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes were refreshed to keep GATE-002 deterministic and consistent with current content.

Plans/00-plans-index.md was updated to register the new Provider_Stream_Mapping_External_Reference_A2A.md document. Spec Lock canonical_ssot_hashes refreshed so verify-spec-lock (GATE-002) remains deterministic and passing.
Normalizer pipeline detected sha256 drift for 3 SSOT files after upstream plan edits; refreshing hashes to match current file bytes.

Spec Lock sha256 drift detected for Plans/chain-wizard-flexibility.md, Plans/interview-subagent-integration.md after REPAIR edits added missing ContractRef blocks. Refreshing hashes to match current file contents.

Spec lock drift detected during normalizer run rewrite-20260304-222659-assistant-smart-retrieval-project-search-context-lens; refreshed canonical SSOT sha256 values to current bytes and recorded deterministic update.

Spec lock drift detected during normalizer run rewrite-20260306-220950-persona-system-provider-capability-resolution; refreshed canonical SSOT sha256 values to current bytes and recorded the deterministic update.

Canonical SSOT docs changed during the fleet feature-audit merge, shard normalization, and ContractRef remediation. Decision_Policy requires refreshing Spec Lock hashes and logging the deterministic maintenance action so GATE-002 remains consistent with the current SSOT state.
verify-spec-lock reported canonical SSOT hash drift after normalization updates, so Spec_Lock.json was refreshed deterministically and recorded per policy.
verify-spec-lock detected canonical SSOT hash drift after this rewrite run; refresh Spec Lock sha256 values and record the deterministic repair so gate evaluation remains authoritative.
verify-spec-lock reported canonical SSOT hash drift, so the lockfile hashes were refreshed deterministically and logged per the Spec Lock update protocol.
Verifier-required drift updated canonical SSOT hashes after the rewrite normalization pass, so Spec_Lock.json was refreshed deterministically and recorded.
verify-spec-lock detected canonical SSOT hash drift, so Spec_Lock.json was refreshed deterministically and the verifier-required repair was logged per policy.
